entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary crop studied for yield and response to soil conservation measures.

entities.name: SOUTHWEST ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The region in Ethiopia where the study was conducted.

entities.name: BOKOLE-KARETHA WATERSHED
entities.description: The specific watershed in southwest Ethiopia where field experiments were performed.

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality due to erosion, challenging crop production.

entities.name: WATER EROSION
entities.description: The process of soil removal by water, leading to soil degradation.

entities.name: SURFACE RUNOFF
entities.description: The flow of water that occurs when excess stormwater runs off the land surface.

entities.name: SOIL BUNDS
entities.description: Conservation structures constructed along contour lines to limit surface runoff and soil erosion.

entities.name: TRENCHES
entities.description: Structures often used in combination with soil bunds to manage water runoff.

entities.name: AQUACROP
entities.description: An FAO crop simulation model used to evaluate the effects of soil bunds on runoff and yield.

entities.name: MAIZE YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area of maize.

entities.name: INFILTRATION CAPACITY
entities.description: The maximum rate at which soil can absorb water, related to the curve number in AquaCrop.

entities.name: WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a crop uses water to produce biomass or grain.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of the soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of nutrients to the soil to prevent degradation and nutrient stress.

entities.name: NUTRIENT STRESS
entities.description: A state where plants lack sufficient nutrients for optimal growth.
